content.claim: As of 2024, Hungary's selective grammar-school admissions policies remain politically contested, with the new education minister proposing reforms to reduce memorization and student workload while facing Fidesz criticism over changes to selective admissions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Current policy proposals are not finalized or legislated
Outcome and implementation sequence of any proposed changes remain uncertain
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
